ground.what_this_is: This is the empty starting screen for the A1 Canvas, a workspace where you begin building your workflow from scratch.
ground.what_you_can_do: You can drag a node from the left-hand palette onto the central stage to start composing your workflow, or click the 'Start Demo' button to run the system's built-in example.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the toolbar region (canvas/app/src/regions/Toolbar.tsx); adding or changing a layer control is a code change there, surfaced via the build-intent wire.
howto.what: The layers control on the toolbar.
howto.what_you_can_do: Open the layers panel to manage the structural depth of the current workspace.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the toolbar region (canvas/app/src/regions/Toolbar.tsx); changing its behavior is a code change there, surfaced via the build-intent wire.
howto.what: The wire command button on the bridge's command strip.
howto.what_you_can_do: Click this to initiate a wire operation, translating the board's current state into a dispatchable intent for the system to execute.

howto.how_to_change: The rack fills itself from the live registry (/object_info); adding a node-type (nodes/<name>.py) makes a new block appear here automatically, no palette code.
howto.what: The Node palette — the rack of build-blocks (every node-type the system knows, grouped by kind: process · acts, content · holds).
howto.what_you_can_do: Click a block to drop that node on the board; select two and use + wire to connect them.

howto.how_to_change: Mockup-only; no built code path maps to this specific text yet.
howto.what: The empty stage instruction telling the operator to begin building.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the instruction to understand how to start composing the workflow by dragging a node from the palette.

howto.how_to_change: The block's appearance and label are defined in the Palette.tsx region; however, the actual node type 'Content' is a live registry entry (nodes/<name>.py). Adding or modifying this node type requires updating the registry definition, not the palette code itself.
howto.what: A draggable tile representing a 'Content' node type (ID 5) available for building workflows.
howto.what_you_can_do: Drag this block from the palette onto the central canvas to instantiate a Content node in your workflow, or select it to inspect its properties.

dossier.represents: A content node block in the palette rack

howto.how_to_change: Mockup-only; no built code path maps to this specific count display yet.
howto.what: The node count display on the graph chip.
howto.what_you_can_do: See how many nodes currently exist in the codebase view; it updates as you drag nodes from the palette or wire them together.

howto.how_to_change: Mockup-only; no built code path maps to this specific empty state yet. It lives conceptually in the canvas region (canvas/app/src/NodeShape.tsx), but changes are currently external-agent driven.
howto.what: The empty starting screen for the A1 Canvas, the workspace where you begin building your workflow from scratch.
howto.what_you_can_do: Drag a node from the left-hand palette onto the central stage to start composing your workflow, or click the 'Start Demo' button to run the system's built-in example.

howto.how_to_change: This text is driven by the NodeShape.tsx region (specifically the NodeShapeUtil utility) and the App.tsx shell; changing the instruction text requires a code edit in that region.
howto.what: The J1 instruction banner on the canvas, announcing the core workflow loop.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the instructions to understand the starting action: drag a node, wire connections, and run the system.

howto.how_to_change: Mockup-only; no built code path maps to it yet.
howto.what: The 'Open the demo' button on the empty canvas.
howto.what_you_can_do: Launch the system's built-in example workflow to see the system in action.

howto.how_to_change: This is a mockup-only control; no built code path maps to it yet.
howto.what: A control on the canvas that initiates a dialogue with the system's own source code.
howto.what_you_can_do: Click it to ask a question of the system's own source code and receive an answer derived from that codebase.

howto.how_to_change: The indicator is the activity region (runtime/suite.py:now); changing its state or text is a code change there.
howto.what: The presence indicator on the activity feed, showing the system's current state.
howto.what_you_can_do: See the system's live state at a glance (empty vs. active) and understand the current graph status without reading code.

howto.how_to_change: This message is rendered by the canvas region (canvas/app/src/NodeShape.tsx); changing the text or styling requires a code edit there.
howto.what: The empty state message displayed on the canvas when no nodes are selected or present.
howto.what_you_can_do: It informs the operator that the workspace is currently empty and ready for the first action (dragging a node or starting a demo).

howto.how_to_change: The panel region is defined in canvas/app/src/regions/Inspector.tsx; changing its fields or structure requires code edits there.
howto.what: The Inspector panel on the canvas — the control surface for viewing and configuring a selected node.
howto.what_you_can_do: Select a node on the board to view its configuration fields, typed inputs, and outputs here; the panel reflects the live state of that node.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the toolbar region (canvas/app/src/regions/Toolbar.tsx); changing the state or the light is a code change there, surfaced via the build-intent wire.
howto.what: The live presence light on the toolbar's command strip.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the system's current state at a glance (running, thinking, or awaiting) and interact with the system's live presence.

reasons: accuracy jury (Layer 2, SOFT — variance not error): no quorum that the dossier is grounded in the element (0 of 3 draws agreed grounded). Flagged for operator scrutiny.

howto.what: The force control on the toolbar.
howto.what_you_can_do: Click this to immediately trigger a forced system action, overriding normal state checks.

reasons: accuracy jury (Layer 2, SOFT — variance not error): no quorum that the dossier is grounded in the element (1 of 3 draws agreed grounded). Flagged for operator scrutiny.

howto.how_to_change: The rail is driven by NodeShape.tsx (NodeShapeUtil) and the App.tsx shell; adding or changing a node type requires a code edit there.
howto.what: The left-hand rail containing the draggable node types for the canvas.
howto.what_you_can_do: Drag a node from this palette onto the stage to begin composing your workflow; the rail reflects the system's available node kinds.

reasons: accuracy jury (Layer 2, SOFT — variance not error): no quorum that the dossier is grounded in the element (1 of 3 draws agreed grounded). Flagged for operator scrutiny.

howto.how_to_change: This is a mockup-only annotation; changing it requires editing the NodeShape.tsx region to alter the visual mapping logic.
howto.what: A static annotation on the canvas board stating that a node's visual 'colour' corresponds to its logical 'kind'.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the rule to understand how the system distinguishes between different types of nodes visually; this is a declarative statement, not an interactive control.
